jobDescription: We are notonthehighstreet. Home to over 5500 of the UK’s best small creative businesses, we have been changing the online retail landscape since 2006. Whether you’re working with our Partners to help grow their business, or behind the scenes developing code, we are continuously innovating & working on some really cool stuff all at the same time. And the best bit? We’re making a difference to our Partners’ lives as we do it. We’re about to embark on an ambitious new phase of development to advance our technology, including platform re-architecture to serverless microservices, rewriting the front-end and introducing new big data & machine learning platforms.  It’s an exciting time and we need to strengthen and grow our teams! As our Android Software Engineer, you will inspire others and champion what can be achieved with mobile. You should be keen to share ideas, expertise and best practice about how we can improve our existing native applications as well as contributing ideas that can help us deliver a first class mobile experience. Some of the things you'll be doing Develop and maintain new features for our existing native Android app as well as any new apps that might be required in the future You’ll keep on top of changes on the Android development platform and ecosystem You’ll be quick to learn and open to learning new technologies and languages as required You’ll work collaboratively with other engineers in the team, UX designers, Product Manager and Technical Lead to inform the product’s roadmap, and implement the features and improvements required to bring the Android app to as many people as possible You’ll work with the i OS app engineers when needed to find common cross-platform solutions. You’ll write code that is testable, well architected, efficient and documented You’ll use common design patterns and make the best use of the programming language to craft the best possible solution You’ll write and maintain tests in the form of Unit and UI Tests, using any tool deemed appropriate by the team You’ll be keen on sharing Android development expertise with the wider technical team You’ll Pair- Program and work side-by-side with other Android engineers and i OS engineers when required Some key skills + experience you’ll need You’ll have experience developing Android apps in a commercial environment You’ll have experience with Java and keep up-to-date with the latest changes to the language. Experience with Kotlin is desirable but not required You have experience of working with modular architecture, and common software design patterns i.e. MVC, MVVM, MVP etc. You’ll have experience with using Gradle to build your projects and manage dependencies You’ll have a good understanding of Networking, Concurrency, and Memory Management on Android You have good knowledge of Web Services and RESTful API Design You understand the Android Design Guidelines and Material Design You’ll have experience in using Git and Git Hub to manage source-control You’ll have experience in Agile methodologies. You will be open and collaborative, with the ability to effectively communicate native technology and constraints to technical and non-technical teams alike Experience with automation tools like Continuous Integration systems and Continuous Deployment tools is desirable but not required Experience with Test- Driven Development, Unit Tests and UI Tests is desirable but not required Experience with Behaviour- Driven Development is desirable but not required Some knowledge or experience with Swift and i OS app development is desirable but not required Experience in Agile Lean principles is desirable but not required What we can offer you Because we know how important learning is, we provide the Tech team with online training and time set aside to complete course work, guilds sessions, hackathons and learning groups. On top of this, we offer plenty of fun stuff - staff discount, birthday vouchers, soft/hot drinks on tap, regular Friday drinks and of course our renowned Christmas and Summer parties. As the home of thoughtful gifts, ‘thoughtful’ is our middle name so we also love a surprise or two… (but that’s enough on that for now!) Our perks It’s important to us that our people are well looked after, which is why we offer everyone BUPA healthcare, life insurance and a pension plan. Because having a break is important for your physical and mental wellbeing, our holiday allowance is 25 days (plus bank holidays) AND your entitlement will increase an extra day for every 2 years you spend with us.
